channel.title: Posts on Amin's Den
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Amin's Den

item.title: Optimized Motion Blur
item.description: Recently, I wanted to explore different Motion Blur techniques being used in the Video Game Industry. Now, I have decided to write down the result of this short research of mine. At the first glance, I found several per-pixel motion blur approaches, but they did not impress me. To explain why I was not satisfied with the results, some history lessons are required! Screen-space Motion Blur My main issue with the trivial approaches was that it would only take the camera motion into account.

item.title: Mithra Graphics Renderer
item.description: “Mithra” is a physically-based graphics renderer featuring many new and modern rendering techniques such as image-based lighting, shadow-mapping, etc. I have also proposed and defended this renderer as my B.Sc. project at Amirkabir University of Technology (Tehran Polytechnic). This is a showcase of some of its main features. Hopefully, in near future, I will share more about the development process in this blog.‍ #slide-window { position: relative; width: 700px; height: 350px; overflow: hidden; top: 0px; left: 0px; } #slides-list { width: 700px; height: 350px; position: absolute; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; -webkit-transform: translate3d(0px, 0px, 0px); transform: translate3d(0px, 0px, 0px); transition: all 0.

item.title: My journey at ARC
item.description: While I was a researcher at Amirkabir University of Technology Robotic Center, I’ve developed a simulation environment using Unity3D game engine to connect to ROS framework. This tool was mostly used to test our AI, movement, localization, and vision systems. We needed this environment because other teams such as mechanic or electronic team would want to take robots to work on them and at the same time software team needed to continue their development plans.
